Gizem Barreto Martins received her BA, MA, and PhD degrees in American Culture and Literature from Istanbul University. Following her graduate studies, she held academic positions as a research assistant, instructor, and lecturer at various institutions, while also working as a freelance translator of academic and literary texts.

Her MA thesis focused on the relationship between gender and language in the plays of David Mamet. In her PhD dissertation, she examined the intersection of theatre and politics through selected works from American drama, drawing on the theoretical frameworks of Gilles Deleuze and Félix Guattari.

Her research interests include modern and postmodern literature, theatre theory and criticism, dramatic texts, and poststructuralist literary theory.
